Apply: active-pane conversion, decide NULL ONE question — “≥1 pattern alternative match DEFAULT-VISIBLE pane innerText?”; yes (even via incidental static text overclaiming test name), KEEP genuine regex verbatim (rescope , flag dragon comment, never rename/retarget); NULL nothing default-visible satisfies faithful pattern (req()-gated output). content-length tautology regex still gets real anchor pane unconditional guidance HTML. Run pre-gate adversarial refutation precisely can CORRECT map, just confirm — value peaks refutedCount>0; adopt corrections, verify critic firsthand, gate. Census actual blocks; predecessor’s per-block anticipation hint, spec.

what: Learning 42 — Genetic-Value family run-and-observe conversion (8e-3 part B-1, FIRST 8e-3 cut): a tautology that has a DEAD computed grepl pattern → REVIVE that exact pattern rescoped (vs #41’s fresh-anchor case); the pre-gate refutation NARROWS a revived pattern by pruning FOREIGN alternatives while keeping real-but-data-dependent ones; and a missing-NOT_CRAN regression read fakes a 40-pass “regression” (S42, Phase 8e-3 Genetic-Value family / issue #40)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: Converted 3 Genetic-Value-family E2E files (genetic-value-module 7 / -detailed 7 / -tutorial 8 = 22 browser-booting blocks) content-blind navigate_to_tab → grepl(get_html_safe(app,"body")) idiom assert_active_pane(app,"Genetic Value Analysis",<pattern>), FIRST 8e-3 cut. class #38–#41 — PURE [refactor-] run--observe (defect; GV pane already renders, “Genetic Value Analysis” tabPanel title appUI.R:148 == module h3 modGeneticValue.R:32), gated PRE-RED→run--observe [author-decision]; [mutation-check] supplies rigor (synthetic RED). Outcome: 16 keep-regex · 3 REVIVE · 1 ANCHOR · 2 NULL. () NEW sub-case — tautology dead computed grepl pattern → REVIVE exact pattern, rescoped, fresh anchor (#41(c) handled expect_true(TRUE)/nchar tautologies surviving regex). Three blocks read has_X <- grepl(PATTERN, html, ...) asserted expect_true(TRUE) — author WROTE intended check never asserted . faithful conversion revives PATTERN active pane: D3 founder|equivalent|FE|genetic (✓“founder” guidance “rare founder alleles” + “genetic” h3), D6 report|export|download|summary (✓“Export /Subset” download buttons + “Summary” nested-tab label). remaining content-LENGTH tautology (D7 nchar(html)>200, regex) still gets guidance ANCHOR (“ranks animals”, genetic_value.html) per #41(c). (b) pre-gate refutation NARROWS revived pattern — pruning FOREIGN alternatives KEEPING real--data-dependent ones — distinct correction mode #40d/#41d’s NULL/un-NULL. T8 (revived focal|display|Show.*entries|search|filter) corrected just filter: four alternatives (focal/display/Show.*entries/search) COPY-PASTE artifacts another module’s test appear NOWHERE GV pane, “filter” matches default-visible text (“Filter View”/“Filter IDs”). Contrast D3/D6, whose non-matching alternatives (founder-EQUIVALENTS, report) REAL GV concepts rendered DATA 8e-6 → keep verbatim (faithful author intent). Rule: reviving dead multi-alternative pattern, KEEP alternatives genuine pane concepts (default-visible now, data-bearing output later); DROP alternatives foreign module entirely. genuine, already-passing grepl (16 keeps, incl. 3 dragons M4/D1/T4) stays VERBATIM regardless (#41b) — prune applies REVIVED dead patterns authoring new assertion. (c) 2 NULL (T5 “Value Designation”, T7 “Z-score”) — data-dependent results concepts absent static UI/guidance, faithful default-visible alternative → assert pane-active , defer data-bearing assertion 8e-6 (#40b/#41a). (d) regression read OMITS NOT_CRAN=true fakes regression. first [regression-read] (NOT_CRAN) returned 2122 passed / 159 skipped — 40-pass DROP vs 2162 baseline looked alarming. test-tree-edit e2e files (self-skip create_test_app() helper-shinytest2.R:196) lower non-e2e passes — impossibility TELL. Cause: without NOT_CRAN=true, ~40 NON-e2e unit tests skip_on_cran() guards silently skip (skip ≠ fail, FAIL stayed 0 masqueraded clean--smaller suite). Re-running NOT_CRAN=true restored 2162 / 0 / 0 / 156-skip / 5-warn / 0 offenders EXACTLY, 3 GV files showed 0/0/0/22-skip (proving edit innocent). Reinforces [regression-read]: NOT_CRAN=true mandatory, suspicious pass-count delta edit provably can’t cause MEASUREMENT artifact — diagnose firsthand (re-measure / reason skip mechanism), never hand-wave claim “baseline held” wrong read. (e) Family-per-session split extends 8e-3 (owner-gated). 8e-3 censused firsthand 8 files / ~56 blocks (~3× 8e-2 session, past family boundary S38–S41 set); per plan §5 “may split oversized” + don’t-bundle (FM #18/#25) posed pre-RED scope [author-decision] owner scoped session genetic-value family (breeding-groups family + settings-/workflow deferred follow-ons). Confirms #38’s “split oversized conversion slice” next slice; special menu/workflow files (navbarMenu finalization navigate_to_menu_item, visit-N conversion) distinct higher-value work belongs cut. (f) Phase-3E / scope: test-tree-→ browser run (22 blocks / 22 expectations, 1:1 swap net 0) + live mutation-check spike runtime (#31 pattern). [mutation-check] PASS, INVERTED (Genetic Value Analysis TARGET pane) — correct (Genetic Value Analysis,"Run Analysis")→TRUE, wrong-pane (Pedigree Browser,"Run Analysis")→FALSE, wrong-content (Genetic Value Analysis,"Focal Animals")→FALSE (“Focal Animals” Pedigree-modPedigree.R:52, grep-confirmed foreign GV), old whole-body grepl("Focal Animals",body)→TRUE (content-blind contrast), active-pane innerText grepl→FALSE (sanity). tests/ .lintr-excluded ([lint-net-zero] N/config); R/ change → document()/NEWS ([news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG ). Commit 3 test files + docs via explicit git add ([macos-dupe-scan] — .DS_Store/..Rcheck//PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html must ride along); /tmp/mutation_check_8e3_gv.R committed. Next 8e-3 cuts: breeding-groups family (3 files, ~23) settings-+ workflow-integration (2 files, ~11 — finalize navigate_to_menu_item true visible-pane check, kill workflow visit-N false-positive). Reflexes: [refactor-][author-decision][mutation-check][verify-first][regression-read][right-sized-orchestration][completeness-workflow][phase-3E-smoke][news-vs-changelog][macos-dupe-scan][lint-net-zero]. Apply: active-pane conversion, split OLD assertion’s kind — genuine passing grepl stays VERBATIM (rescope , flag dragons, never rename); tautology carries DEAD computed grepl pattern REVIVES pattern rescoped (prune alternatives FOREIGN module, keep real--data-dependent ones); -regex/content-length tautology gets guidance anchor; NULL nothing default-visible matches faithful pattern. Always read regression NOT_CRAN=true; test-edit appears drop pass count, suspect missing NOT_CRAN (another measurement artifact) suspecting real regression — re-measure firsthand. slice ~3× session, pose pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion one family.

what: Learning 43 — Breeding-Groups family run-and-observe conversion (8e-3 part B-2): a THIRD NULL sub-case — a STATIC feature that is present-but-in-an-inactive-NESTED-tab (display:none) → NULL+defer (vs #39’s nonexistent-feature and #40’s data-dependent req()-gate); nested-tab NAV labels ARE in active-pane innerText while their CONTENT is not; and sum(nb)−sum(failed) is NOT the passed count (S43, Phase 8e-3 Breeding-Groups family / issue #40)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: Converted 3 Breeding-Groups-family E2E files (breeding-groups-module 7 / -detailed 7 / -tutorial 9 = 23 browser-booting blocks) content-blind navigate_to_tab → grepl(get_html_safe(app,"body")) idiom assert_active_pane(app,"Breeding Groups",<pattern>), 2nd three 8e-3 cuts. class #38–#42 — PURE [refactor-] run--observe (defect; BG pane already renders, “Breeding Groups” tabPanel title appUI.R:166), gated PRE-RED→run--observe [author-decision]; [mutation-check] supplies rigor (synthetic RED). Outcome: 12 keep-regex · 6 REVIVE · 1 ANCHOR · 4 NULL. () THIRD NULL sub-case — STATICALLY-PRESENT control lives INACTIVE nested tab (display:none) active-pane innerText → NULL-pane-active + defer. modBreedingGroups right column nested tabsetPanel “Groups”(default)/“Statistics”/“Group Detail”; export downloadButtons “Export Current Group”/“Export Current Group Kinship Matrix” (modBreedingGroups.R:83-86) STATIC (req()-gated) sit INACTIVE “Group Detail” nested tab → innerText (honors CSS visibility) excludes , guidance HTML export tokens. D5/T7 (“export”) T9 (“kinship matrix export”) faithful default-visible match → NULL, deferred 8e-6 / nested-tab-navigation enhancement. DISTINCT #39b (NONEXISTENT feature — T5 “infants dam”: control modular UI , tutorial-) #40b (DATA-DEPENDENT req()-gated output). One family carried two different NULL reasons (T5 nonexistent; D5/T7/T9 present--nested-hidden) — correctly resolve pane-active-, different reasons worth recording: NULL test still purely “≥1 faithful alternative match DEFAULT-VISIBLE innerText?” (#41a), “default-visible” excludes inactive nested-tab content firmly excludes req()-gated empty div. (b) Nested-tab NAV labels active-pane innerText even though inactive tabs’ CONTENT . nested <ul class="nav nav-tabs"> (labels “Groups”/“Statistics”/“Group Detail”) descendant top-level BG .tab-pane always visible, M7 statistic|summary|total validly anchors “Statistics” nav label D4’s revived “group” matches “Groups” nav label — even though tabs’ bodies hidden. one genuinely uncertain point (M7’s matcher nav label — “statistic” sidebar control guidance); pre-gate critic settled RENDERING actual Shiny navbarPage+nested-tabsetPanel DOM via Rscript (stronger firsthand check source-reasoning) confirming nav <ul> p.innerText; browser run confirmed M7 GREEN. Lesson: nested-tab NAV labels usable static anchors; nested-tab CONTENT (display:none tab inactive) — active-vs-inactive split #39 found Input module’s nested tabs, now stated general rule module whose named features hide behind non-default nested tabs. (c) REVIVE-pruning recurred (#42b) inputId artifacts named prune class. 6 tautologies--dead-grepl revived: D2→harem, D4→result|group|table|output|formed (keep — “group” matches now, rest data-dependent formed-group display → 8e-6), T1→group.*formation|source.*animal (prune framing words workflow/Choose.*group), T4→Seed.*Group|seed.*animal|specific.*animal (prune pre.*seed + inputId seedGroups), T6→Include.*kinship|kinship.*display (prune inputId showKinship + non-matching-order display.*kinship), T8→top.*ranked (prune high.*value/value.*animal never-rendered + FOREIGN-module token genetic.*analysis — GV pane). Prune classes, confirmed critic: inputId artifacts (seedGroups/showKinship/sexRatio attributes, innerText), never-rendered framing words, wrong-token-order .* alternatives, foreign-module tokens; KEEP matching label + real--data-dependent (#42a/b). genuine passing grepl (12 keeps incl. D1 dragon size|number|count|animals — “size” control, matches via “number”/“animals”) stays VERBATIM, flagged comment, never renamed (#41b). (d) pre-gate refutation CONFIRMED whole map (0 corrections, like S40) yet still earned keep — RESOLVING real uncertainty, just rubber-stamping. Unlike S41/S42 (corrected 2/12 1/22), 0/23 changed — workflow’s value settling M7 firsthand (DOM render) confidence lowest, dismissing two skeptic refutations demanded NULL false premise nested nav labels innerText. Robust partial agent failure: 2 3 skeptics hit stream-idle timeouts; 1 complete skeptic + 1 partial (whose returned refutations critic still adjudicated) + critic sufficient — [completeness-workflow] degrades gracefully synthesizer verifies firsthand rather vote-counting. 0-correction pre-gate wasted converts private low-confidence judgment (M7) DOM-verified one slow 23-Chrome browser run. (e) passed = sum(res$nb) − sum(res$failed) WRONG — refines [regression-read]. first read reported passed=2323, +161 jump S40–S42 baseline 2162 — impossible test-e2e edit (BG files self-skip), per #42d diagnosed firsthand rather reporting regression hand-waving “baseline held”. Cause: nb column counts skip warning rows — canonical tally (counting expectation classes directly: expectation_success=2162, expectation_skip=156, expectation_warning=5; 2162+156+5=2323) shows true passed count expectation_success (== testthat reporter’s PASS line), sum(nb)−sum(failed). S40–S42 “2162” canonical count; formula artifact. Report passed via expectation_success (reporter PASS line), never sum(nb). Baseline held EXACTLY: 2162 / 0 failed / 0 error / 156 skip / 5 pre-existing modPyramid warn / 0 non-e2e offenders. (f) Phase-3E / scope: test-tree-→ browser run (23 blocks / 23 expectations, 1:1 swap net 0) + live mutation-check spike runtime (#31 pattern). [mutation-check] PASS, INVERTED (Breeding Groups TARGET pane) — correct (Breeding Groups,"Form Groups")→TRUE, wrong-pane (Pedigree Browser,"Form Groups")→FALSE, wrong-content (Breeding Groups,"Focal Animals")→FALSE (“Focal Animals” Pedigree/Input-modPedigree.R:52/modInput.R:114, grep-confirmed foreign BG; “Form Groups” BG actionButton modBreedingGroups.R:66), old whole-body grepl("Focal Animals",body)→TRUE (content-blind contrast), active-pane innerText grepl→FALSE (sanity). tests/ .lintr-excluded ([lint-net-zero] N/config); R/ change → document()/NEWS ([news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG ). Commit 3 test files + docs via explicit git add ([macos-dupe-scan] — .DS_Store/..Rcheck//PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html must ride along; * 2.* macOS dupes .Rproj.user/, none tests/); /tmp/mutation_check_8e3_bg.R committed. 8e-3 now 2/3 done (genetic-value S42 + breeding-groups S43); LAST 8e-3 cut = settings-(4) + workflow-integration (7) — mechanical: finalize navigate_to_menu_item true visible-pane check (resolve §8.3 navbarMenu false-positive, update docstring helper-shinytest2.R:283-299) + convert workflow “visits N tabs” loop per-pane active assertions; SEPARATE session (FM #18/#25). Reflexes: [refactor-][author-decision][mutation-check][verify-first][regression-read][right-sized-orchestration][completeness-workflow][phase-3E-smoke][news-vs-changelog][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: active-pane conversion, NULL block nothing DEFAULT-VISIBLE matches faithful pattern — “default-visible” excludes req()-gated content static control lives INACTIVE nested tab (display:none), distinct nonexistent feature; three resolve pane-active-+ defer. Nested-tab NAV labels (always visible) usable anchors; nested-tab CONTENT . reviving dead pattern, prune inputId artifacts / framing words / wrong-order .* / foreign-module tokens, keep matching label + real--data-dependent. Report regression passed count expectation_success (testthat PASS line), NEVER sum(nb)−sum(failed) (nb includes skips+warnings). Run pre-gate refutation even expecting 0 corrections — payoff can resolving ONE low-confidence judgment firsthand (render actual DOM); degrades gracefully skeptics time , provided critic verifies firsthand.

what: Learning 44 — Settings-About + Workflow-Integration conversion (8e-3 FINAL cut → 8e-3 COMPLETE): the navbarMenu(“More”) dragon is RESOLVED firsthand (a child DOES become the lone active top-level .tab-pane via set_inputs(mainNavbar=child)) → navigate_to_menu_item finalized as a genuine visible-pane check (docstring only, body unchanged); navbar CHROME (brand) is element-scopable (strictly stronger than the 8e-2 whole-body carve-out); and a “visits N tabs” count-loop tautology only discriminates when the threshold is raised to the FULL count (S44, Phase 8e-3 FINAL / issue #40)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text:
Converted LAST two 8e-3 files — test-e2e-settings-.R (4) + test-e2e-workflow-integration.R (7) = 11 browser-booting blocks — content-blind navigate_to_tab → grepl(get_html_safe(app,"body")) idiom assert_active_pane(...). class #38–#43 — PURE [refactor-] run--observe, gated PRE-RED→run--observe [author-decision]; [mutation-check] supplies rigor (synthetic RED). Outcome: 10 keep-regex-rescope · 1 navbar-chrome carve-. () navbarMenu(“”) dragon — flagged R1/§2.3-item-4, deferred 8e-1 ([hard-gate-spike] #37 confirmed input read-back) carried 🐉 S42/S43 — RESOLVED firsthand: navbarMenu child become lone active top-level .tab-pane via set_inputs(mainNavbar=child). live-DOM spike (Rscript→AppDriver) confirmed navigate_to_menu_item(app,"Settings"): top-level .tab-content count == 1 (children land single container 8 top-level tabPanels — separate dropdown container), get_active_pane_value==“Settings”, get_active_pane_text==“Application Settings Configuration options go ”; idem (version/credits/NIH) Help (Documentation/Online). navigate_to_menu_item’s delegate body (→navigate_to_tab’s set_inputs+read-back) ALREADY genuine visible-pane switch — docstring’s “body-grepl passes regardless true visible-pane switch (shallow-coverage limit … strengthened 8e)” caveat needed retiring (helper-shinytest2.R:283-292; body unchanged → still PURE run--observe, helper RED→GREEN). [hard-gate-spike] CONFIRM mode (vs 8e-1’s FALSIFY): spike can validate planned mechanism, break — still RUN firsthand dependent conversions (rendering DOM strongest refutation load-bearing question DOM behavior, separate source-reasoning skeptic panel — S43’s pre-gate shape — SUBSUMED two live spikes). (b) Navbar CHROME (brand/title) element-scopable — strictly STRONGER choice 8e-2 whole-body carve-— even though active-pane apply . brand renders GeneKeepR, OUTSIDE .tab-pane (spike-confirmed !document.querySelector('.tab-pane .navbar-brand')), assert_active_pane correctly reach (category home-navigation navbar-LABEL carve-outs :147-185 8e-2 left whole-body grepl). unlike labels, brand stable class → scope grepl element : grepl("GeneKeepR", get_html_safe(app, ".navbar-brand")). [mutation-check]-proven strictly stronger: grepl("Breeding", brand)==FALSE OLD grepl("Breeding", body)==TRUE (whole-body content-blind — “GeneKeepR” also lives Home

“Welcome GeneKeepR” pane, old assertion passed even brand removed). Rule: non-pane navbar element stable selector → scope selector, don’t settle whole-body grepl carve-. (c) “visits N tabs” COUNT-LOOP tautology becomes discriminating threshold raised FULL count. Workflow W1 looped 6 panes accumulating tabs_visited asserted >= 3 (content-blind whole-body grepls → always 6 → always passes). Converting inner check assert_active_pane necessary sufficient: >= 3, breaking ONE nav drops count 5 STILL passes — mutation doesn’t bite. Raise threshold equal count (== 6L) single failed/wrong-pane nav reds block (mutation-confirmed: wrong-pane assert_active_pane→FALSE → count 5 → ==6L fails). one block AGGREGATES many panes (vs per-block 1:1 swaps elsewhere); discriminating lever THRESHOLD, just inner predicate. (d) GOTCHA — re-navigating ALREADY-ACTIVE tab raises shinytest2’s “Server update output values within 4 seconds” (set_inputs(mainNavbar=) default wait_=TRUE blocks output flush -op nav never produces). Harmless real isolated blocks (fresh app navigates Home→DISTINCT tab, W1/W3’s sequences distinct-adjacent transitions → output always updates), bit FIRST diagnostic spike checked two patterns pane back--back. spiking multiple checks one pane single app instance, sequence DISTINCT tabs reads (pass wait_=FALSE); defect converted tests. (e) W2/W3 .list() responsiveness tautologies (grepl) — strengthened genuine pane-switch asserts (Input-active--Home-active; final-pane-active 4-switch loop), net-0 expectations, dropping now-unused get_values_safe() calls. 8 remaining workflow patterns + 4 settings patterns genuine grepls → KEEP verbatim, rescope (every one [verify-first]-confirmed real active-pane innerText spike v2: ALL_OK). (f) Phase-3E / scope: test-tree-→ browser run (11 blocks / 12 expectations — net 0 vs old 12: settings 4 + workflow W1:1/W2:2/W3:1/W4:1/W5:1/W6:1/W7:1) + two live DOM spikes + live mutation-check spike runtime (#31 pattern). [regression-read] non-e2e 2162 expectation_success / 0 failed / 0 error / 156 skip / 5 pre-existing modPyramid warn / 0 non-e2e offenders — S40–S43 baseline held EXACTLY (read via sum(nb)−failed−skipped−warning=2162, sum(nb)=2323, per #43e). tests/ .lintr-excluded ([lint-net-zero] N/config); helper docstring TEST-tree roxygen comment, R/ export → document()/NEWS ([news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG ). Commit 3 test-tree files + docs via explicit git add ([macos-dupe-scan] — .DS_Store/..Rcheck//PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html must ride along); spikes /tmp/spike_navbarmenu_8e3.R, /tmp/spike_workflow_patterns.R, /tmp/mutation_check_8e3_final.R committed. 8e-3 now COMPLETE (genetic-value S42 + breeding-groups S43 + settings-/workflow S44); next 8e-4 (namespace input-→dataInput- fix + error-states/boundary-conditions interaction revival — RED→GREEN ns fix, §2.4) — SEPARATE session (FM #18/#25). Reflexes: [hard-gate-spike][refactor-][author-decision][mutation-check][verify-first][regression-read][phase-3E-smoke][news-vs-changelog][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: plan’s load-bearing assumption DOM behavior, settle RENDERING live DOM bounded spike dependent conversions — [hard-gate-spike] confirms--corrects, live-DOM render subsumes source-reasoning skeptic panel DOM question. set_inputs(mainNavbar=child) activate navbarMenu child lone top-level .tab-pane (one .tab-content), navigate_to_menu_item genuine visible-pane check (docstring-finalization). Scope non-pane navbar element (brand) stable selector, whole-body grepl. Convert “visits N tabs” count-loop raising threshold FULL count single failed nav reds block. Re-navigating already-active tab raises 4s -output timeout — sequence distinct tabs spiking -pane checks one app instance.

what: Learning 45 — Namespace fix + error-states/boundary interaction revival (8e-4): the FIRST hybrid 8e slice (RED→GREEN namespace + run-and-observe conversions, two gates in one session); the namespace discriminator is the input VALUE READ-BACK, not a throw (shinytest2 set_inputs on an unbound id WARNS and never sets); a no-file getData click surfaces a TRANSIENT showNotification toast (the only pre-data error-state); a browser-free recording-stub anchors the helper fix in the always-run layer; and a concurrent external code-formatter rewrote 14 R/ files mid-session — surfaced, owner-gated, verified inert, excluded from the commit (S45, Phase 8e-4 / issue #40)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: Converted test-e2e-error-states.R (13) + test-e2e-boundary-conditions.R (13) = 26 browser-booting blocks content-blind nchar(html)>100 / dead-grepl / interaction-noop-tryCatch idioms behavioral assertions, applied §2.4 namespace fix (input-→dataInput-) 5 sites + upload_and_wait helper. () FIRST 8e slice pure run--observe — HYBRID: RED→GREEN namespace fix (4 tests) + run--observe 23 conversions, gated (PRE-RED→RED RED→GREEN, two [author-decision] gates). Sites per §2.4 (helper-shinytest2.R:150/:154, error-states:24/:45, boundary:43); §2.4 --CHANGE list (data-ready bare ids, data-module="input" literal, #goto_input, tab anchors [data-value=]) respected. (b) namespace discriminator input VALUE READ-BACK, exception. Contrary plan’s “wrong id throws” framing, shinytest2 set_inputs(\input-minParentAge`=…)UNBOUND id emits WARNING ("Unable find input binding") **set anything** (raise R error) — wrong-id RED proven byget_value(input=“dataInput-minParentAge”)staying default "2.0" ≠ set value, atryCatch-caught throw. CLICK path differs:app$click(selector="#input-getData")` missing selector THROWS ("find HTML element") → `click_element_safe`→FALSE → `expect_true` fails. One slice, two distinct namespace discriminators (textInput → read-back; actionButton click → throw→FALSE). **(c) -file `getData` click surfaces TRANSIENT `showNotification` warning toast — error-state available pre-data.** `modInput.R:305-308` `observeEvent(getData)`: `(.null(activeFile())) { showNotification("Please select file first.", type="warning"); return() }`. `[verify-first]` spike confirmed toast renders `#shiny-notification-panel` readable right `click + wait_for_idle` (~5s auto-dismiss). Crucially zero/non-numeric-age blocks WITHOUT file surface error — `minParentAge` `textInput` accepting string (`get_value` reflects "0"/"abc"); `.numeric()` coercion fires inside never-reached (-file) handler. genuine assertions **input round-trip + pane-active**, "error-state" (refines plan's "surfaces visible error/state" — true -file click). **(d) browser-free recording-stub anchors helper namespace fix ALWAYS-RUN layer.** e2e blocks self-skip without `NPRC_RUN_E2E`, `upload_and_wait` exercised 8e-4 test (serves 8e-6's real upload) — RED→GREEN recording fake-app stub `test_helper_shinytest2.R` (mirrors 8a's stub idiom) asserting uploads `dataInput-pedigreeFileOne` clicks `dataInput-getData`; RED = hardcoded `input-pedigreeFileOne` + default `module_id="input"`, GREEN = default `"dataInput"` + DERIVE upload id via `.call(app$upload_file, setNames(list(file_path), sprintf(“%s-%s”, module_id, file_input_id))). +4 always-run expectations (2162→2166) protect fix even browser suite opted . **(e) ZERO blocks deferred 8e-6 — plan's "boundary blocks need data → defer" materialize.** Every pane's CONTROL labels static (present without data) 26 patterns[verify-first]-confirmed real active-pane innerText (every spike cell TRUE): Input (Minimum Parent Age/Read Check Pedigree), Pedigree (Focal Animals/Display Options/search), Pyramid (Bin Size/Age Unit/Age Label), GV (Run Analysis/Iterations/Threshold/Export /Export Subset), BG (Form Groups/Number groups/Sex ratio/Harem), Home (Welcome/GeneKeepR). two rapid/repeat-click blocks assert FINAL pane (Home / Input); narrow/short-window blocks assert Home active BOOT (nav). **(f) ⚠ concurrent external code-formatter rewrote 14R/PRODUCTION files mid-session — surfaced, owner-gated, verified inert, EXCLUDED commit (FM #22 wild).** Orientation showed clean tree (.DS_Store); minutes laterpkgload::load_allFAILED ([verify-first]caught ) parse error inmakeFounderStatsTable.R:68— automated style pass (‘…’→“…”,0→0L,round(x,2)→round(x,2L); likely editor format--save /styler/ another agent open #30 lint work) converted single-quoted HTML strings double quotes WITHOUT escaping innerclass=“…”, closing literal early. modified set actively GROWING (11→13→15→17 tracked files across read-polls) → tool running CONCURRENTLY. Per SAFEGUARDS (preserve user edits) / FM #22 touch, revert, "fix" unauthored uncommitted changes — STOPPED gated owner (AskUserQuestion). next poll formatter SELF-HEALED 2 broken files (later pass rewrote valid) settled 17;[regression-read]proved whole reformat behaviorally inert (non-e2e **2166expectation_success/ 0 failed / 0 error / 156 skip / 5 pre-existingmodPyramidwarn / 0 non-e2e offenders** — S40–S44 baseline + exactly +4 new helper expectations). 8e-4 commit staged 4 test-tree files + docs via explicitgit add, leaving owner's 14-file reformat uncommitted -progress work ([macos-dupe-scan]also excluded.DS_Store/..Rcheck//PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html). Lesson: non-parsing tree START code work always breakage stale checkout — AI/tooling-heavy repo formatter may run concurrently; verify firsthand (load_all+ parse-check +git statusgrowth), changes unauthored + uncommitted, SURFACE + gate, never silently fix revert. **(g)** Phase-3E / scope: test-tree-→ browser run (26 blocks / 29 expectations, 0 failed/0 error/0 skip) + two live spikes (DOM/namespace + mutation-check) runtime (#31). **[mutation-check]** PASS:assert_active_pane(GV,“Export |Export Subset”)→TRUE, wrong-pane(Breeding Groups,…)→FALSE, wrong-content(GV,“Number groups”)→FALSE ("Number groups" BG-, grep-confirmed foreign GV), OLD whole-bodygrepl(“Number groups”, body)→TRUE (content-blind contrast), active-pane innerText grepl→FALSE (sanity); namespacedataInput-minParentAgereflects "0", click#dataInput-getData→notification, wrong selector#input-getData→click_element_safe→FALSE.tests/.lintr-excluded (**[lint-net-zero]** N/); /change → nodocument()/NEWS (**[news-vs-changelog]** → CHANGELOG ). Next **8e-5** (⚠ PRODUCTIONR/gatedset_seed— owner-gated full RED→GREEN→REFACTOR+check(), [production--disguise]) **8e-6** (real upload+QC/GVA/breeding flows + deferred data-bearing asserts; 8e-4 prerequisite — thedataInput-ids now correct). **Reflexes:** [hard-gate-spike][verify-first][mutation-check][regression-read][author-decision][phase-3E-smoke][news-vs-changelog][macos-dupe-scan]. **Apply:** hybrid 8e slice gates twice (PRE-RED→RED failing namespace tests, RED→GREEN). namespaced textInput wrong-id discriminator theget_valueread-back (shinytest2 WARNS, never sets — throw); actionButton theapp$clickthrow. Anchor helper's namespace fix browser-free recording-stub unit test (e2e layer self-skips). Convert pre-data block toassert_active_paneagainst STATIC control label, data-bearing content. Ifload_allfails start code work unauthored uncommittedR/edits still growing, formatter running concurrently — surface + owner-gate, verify inert via regression, commit files via explicitgit add`; never revert silently fix someone else’s -progress work.

what: Learning 46 — Stochastic determinism hook (8e-5): the [production-in-disguise] item EXECUTED — an env/option-gated set_seed() added to two EXPORTED module servers via owner-gated full RED→GREEN→REFACTOR; the determinism RED compares a stochastic engine’s output across TWO testServer runs (RNG carries across invocations) guarded against a vacuous identical(NULL,NULL); committing a concurrent formatter’s reformat DESYNCS man/ (it rewrapped #' comments + changed signature defaults to 8L) so run document() before check(); and the new cross-file helper’s object_usage_linter flag is a [stale-namespace] transient (S46, Phase 8e-5 / issue #40)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: slice flagged #35 [production--disguise] ONE issue-#40 item needs real R/ change now DONE: env/option-gated set_seed() hook added top gvResults’s breedingGroups’s eventReactive bodies (immediately req(), ahead withProgress, intervening RNG consumed engine call) — seed <- getOption("nprcgenekeepr.gva_seed", .integer(Sys.getenv("NPRC_GVA_SEED", NA))); (!.na(seed)) set_seed(seed) (nprcgenekeepr.bg_seed/NPRC_BG_SEED), using existing exported set_seed() (pins sample.kind="Rounding"). (b) determinism RED [discriminating-RED] STOCHASTIC consumer across TWO testServer invocations. set_seed-determinism observed one run; RED sets option runs testServer(modGeneticValueServer, …) TWICE one test, capturing gvResults()[order(id),"gu"] time via <<- (directly-stochastic gu geneDrop→sample; rank column seq_len(n) construction → useless discriminator), asserts expect_identical(guA, guB). FAILS HEAD R’s global RNG STATE CARRIES two sequential testServer runs (per-run reseed) → unseeded gu differs run--run; gated seed makes runs reseed identically → identical. ⚠ Guard vacuous pass: <<- capture silently failed (NULL), identical(NULL,NULL) PASS fake green RED — determinism test FIRST asserts expect_true(length(guA) > 0L); HEAD passes (capture works) identical fails, proving RED real, vacuous (extends [identical-proof]/[discriminating-RED] stochastic-output comparison — cf. #18’s “seeded reportGV byte-identical” SEED thing test). Breeding mirrors session$getReturned()$groups(). (c) gate MECHANISM pinned set_seed MOCK ([testServer-mechanics]): local_mocked_bindings(set_seed = function(seed=1L){recorder$called<-TRUE; recorder$seed<-seed}) (.package arg — matches test-set_seed.R; patches namespace binding BARE-NAME call nested moduleServer→eventReactive resolves mock) proves “called option value” (RED HEAD — never called) env-fallback “NPRC_GVA_SEED read option absent → called 7L” (RED HEAD); default-path guard “option+env unset → called” green--arrival (regression guard, honestly classified — RED). Net 8 tests = 6 genuine RED + 2 guards; RED confirmed firsthand GREEN (synthetic RED). (d) REFACTOR factored duplicated 3-line gate one @noRd helper gatedSeed(optionName, envName) R/set_seed.R; structure-, new tests, [document-zero-delta] held (@noRd ⇒ NAMESPACE/man delta change). (e) ⚠ Committing concurrent formatter’s reformat DESYNCS man/ — run document() check(). S45’s reformat (committed clean baseline d0989408 owner request, re-verified inert 2166 via [regression-read]) ALSO rewrapped #' roxygen comments changed savePlotToFile’s formals defaults integer (width=8L/height=6L/dpi=150L) — document() regenerated 3 man files (appServer, modSummaryStatsServer, savePlotToFile); savePlotToFile \usage 8→8L real codoc drift R CMD check flagged WARNING. [deletion-namespace-fallout] COUSIN reverse: source change (reformat, deletion) silently desyncs GENERATED artifact (man/) content grep see — tell git status showing man/ deltas document(), reformat commit. Attribution confirmed firsthand: roxygen versions match (7.3.2 = 7.3.2), git show d0989408 -- R/appServer.R R/modSummaryStats.R | grep "^[+-]#'" proved reformat rewrapped #' comments → 3 man changes reformat consequences → committed SEPARATE docs: commit (causality explicit), distinct 8e-5 commit. Reflex: committing reformat touches R/, run document() diff man/+NAMESPACE check() — code formatter can edit roxygen comments signature defaults. what: Learning 47 — Real upload→pedigree E2E flow (8e-6a): the FIRST genuinely data-bearing E2E assertions in the suite; the [hard-gate-spike] CORRECTED the plan’s §2.3 “output tier” (get_value(output=DT) un-suspends to non-NULL WITHOUT data → assert rendered-DOM content, not is.null); and a local e2e spike tests the SYSTEM-lib install, not the working tree, unless reinstalled first (S47, Phase 8e-6a / issue #40)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings

Firsthand: pedigree DToutput NULL SUSPENDED (tab hidden); instant navigate_to_tab activates tab UN-SUSPENDS non-NULL json atomic string EVEN studbook (req(pedigreeData()) unmet → empty visibility:hidden div). .null(get_value) discriminates SUSPENSION, DATA → dropped mid-RED asserted RENDERED-DOM content via get_html_safe(app, "#pedigree-pedigreeTable") (→ new reflex [output-dom-discriminator]). what: Learning 48 — Real GVA-run E2E flow (8e-6b): the rankings DT’s value column is real but arrives via orderReport()→rankSubjects()→rbind (NOT reportGV’s cbind — trace column PROVENANCE), and the default topN=20 render truncates to the top-ranked rows so only “High Value” survives (asserting “Low Value”/“Undetermined” would RED a green flow); both data-bearing tokens are seed-INDEPENDENT structural invariants (S48, Phase 8e-6b / issue #40)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: 8e-6b drove real GVA pipeline upload_and_wait(app, obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv) → navigate_to_tab("Genetic Value Analysis") → set nIterations=100 → click_element_safe("#geneticValue-runAnalysis") → wait_for_module_ready("geneticValue") converted 2 NULL’d GV blocks S42 (B1 test-e2e-genetic-value-tutorial.R:99 Value Designation, B2 :144 Z-score) pane-active-data-bearing asserts rendered #geneticValue-rankingsTable — second real-flow slice 8e-6a. what: Learning 49 — Real breeding-group E2E flow (8e-6c → 8e-6 COMPLETE): the export buttons + rendered Group-Detail tables live in a NO-id nested tabset reached only by a DOM click on a[data-value='Group Detail']; make a static-UI button data-bearing by PAIRING its visibility-gated LABEL with a suspendWhenHidden DT so each block needs the full flow (not just nested-tab nav); animalSource='all' isolates breeding from GVA; and the non-e2e baseline is MEASUREMENT-method-sensitive → prove edit-inertness with a stash diff, not the headline count (S49, Phase 8e-6c / issue #40)

what: Learning 50 — CI per-module fresh-process grouping (8e-7, the FINAL §8e slice): partition the 23-file E2E tier into 13 per-module groups each run in a fresh Rscript process so no one process accumulates 23 Chrome instances; the partition is STATICALLY PROVABLE locally (replicate testthat’s stripped-name grepl match against the COMMITTED regexes → exactly 23, no overlap/gap) but the flake fix is LIVE-RUNNER-ONLY; check fail/error BEFORE the silent-skip guard so a real failure is never mislabeled “executed nothing”; and keep the per-group R snippet apostrophe-free because it lives inside bash Rscript -e '...' (S50, Phase 8e-7 / issue #40)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: 8e-7 converted single 23-files--one-process E2E run step .github/workflows/shinytest2.yaml single job looping 13 per-module group regexes, fresh Rscript process, defusing S34 “AppDriver process-count dragon” (23 Chrome instances accumulating one process → ~1 transient timeout / 5 full-tier runs). LAST §8e slice → issue-#40 assertion-strengthening + CI-stability campaign now code-complete. () [author-decision] topology gated, single-job loop chosen matrix. pre-phase AskUserQuestion offered () single job + bash loop spawning one Rscript per group (plan §8’s literal design — 1× setup, sequential, fresh PROCESS per group) vs (B) 13-leg strategy.matrix (fresh RUNNER per group, parallel, per-group UI checks, 13× checkout/setup-r/deps/INSTALL/Chrome cost). Owner chose — cheapest, plan-faithful, root-cause-sufficient (≤3 files/process already defuses dragon); matrix’s extra isolation/visibility wasn’t worth 13× setup nightly job. phase gate PRE-RED→run--observe (CI config, RED→GREEN per plan §6). (b) [ci-static-proof] partition PROVABLE locally even though flake . Per Learning #33c (replicate testthat’s stripped-name grepl match: strip leading test-? + trailing .[rR], grepl(rx, name)) — extracted 13 regexes COMMITTED YAML (draft) proved union==23 ^(app|e2e)- tier overlap/gap/stray, applied FULL 182-file dir (group can’t silently reach non-tier file; ^app- trailing dash correctly excludes test_appServer_dynamicTabs/test_create_test_app). 13 groups: ^app-(2), ^e2e-breeding-groups-(3), ^e2e-genetic-value-(3), ^e2e-input-(3), ^e2e-pedigree-(3), ^e2e-pyramid-(2), + 7 single-file regexes (boundary-conditions, data-ready, error-states, home-navigation, settings-, summary-statistics, workflow-integration). (c) [guard-ordering] check fail/error silent-skip guard. Per group: test_dir(filter=rx, stop_on_failure=FALSE) → cat passed/failed/skipped/error report → (f>0||e>0) quit(status=1) FIRST → (p==0) stop(...). smoke EXPOSED naive order (p==0 guard first) mislabels real failure “executed nothing” whenever failing group 0 passes — reordering makes silent-skip guard fire TRUE nothing-ran case (-skipped / empty) real failures red job correct context. guard now PER GROUP (stronger old whole-run sum(passed)==0): one group’s filter drift can’t hide behind others; ZERO-match regex separately caught test_dir’s “test files found” abort (→ nonzero exit). bash loop runs groups (full signal, one flake doesn’t skip rest) reds job failed — preserving stop_on_failure job semantics; job env / Chrome provisioning / R CMD INSTALL / timeout-minutes:30 / removed continue--error(R6) preserved; group wrapped ::group::/::endgroup:: log folds. (d) [quote-safety] per-group R snippet lives inside bash Rscript -e '...' → ZERO single-quotes allowed. R strings double-quoted inline R COMMENTS apostrophe-free (wrote “groups”/“test_dirs”, “group’s”/“test_dir’s”) — apostrophe terminate bash single-quote break step. Verified grepping -e block ' (none) + bash -n extracted run step (clean) + yaml.safe_load (parses). (e) [phase-3E-smoke] run-step LOGIC locally verifiable; FLAKE . Smoked snippet throwaway test dir (deterministic, Chrome): pass→exit0, fail→exit1 (“executed nothing” mislabel), skip→silent-skip guard→exit1, nomatch→test_dir abort→exit1 — 4 branches. flake mitigation ENVIRONMENTAL / LIVE-RUNNER-(FM #24’s cousin): proven partition selects right files guards behave, first live GitHub run can confirm 23--one-process flake gone — explicitly claimed fixed . ⚠ smoke-HARNESS bug bit briefly: =$(Rscript ... | grep ...) ${PIPESTATUS[0]} reads ASSIGNMENT’s status (always 0), Rscript’s — capture exit $? right non-piped =$(Rscript ...). committed snippet never wrong; harness . (f) [right-tool] deterministic partition best proven executable check, agent fan-— R verification script adversarial proof (asserts -overlap/-gap programmatically); workflow skeptic agents add nothing provably-correct regex partition. (g) Scope = CI-config — shinytest2.yaml + notes; R//tests/ change → suite byte-identical, regression run needed (change alters CI INVOKES suite). [news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG ; [macos-dupe-scan] explicit git add (.DS_Store/..Rcheck//audit-html excluded). push bundle master push (FM #2/#18) — SEPARATE deliverable first exercises two S34 watch items (renv lib-path + now-mitigated flake). Reflexes: [author-decision][ci-static-proof][guard-ordering][quote-safety][phase-3E-smoke][right-tool][regression-read][news-vs-changelog][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: defang per-process resource-accumulation flake CI test tier, partition files per-module groups run FRESH process via bash loop spawning one Rscript per group (preserve env / install / stop_on_failure semantics + PER-GROUP silent-skip guard); PROVE partition locally replicating testthat’s stripped-name grepl match COMMITTED regexes (exactly N, overlap/gap) even though flake fix live-runner-; per-group snippet check fail/error passed==0 guard, keep Rscript -e '...' body apostrophe-free; smoke run-step logic throwaway dir state plainly flake mitigation ships unvalidated first live run.

title: Safeguards
text: AI agents operate well two distinct modes: Engineer Mode — Narrow focus. Implement specific feature, fix specific bug, write specific test. Head , executing clear spec. Architect Mode — Broad focus. Analyze codebase, plan feature, evaluate tradeoffs, design approach. Head , surveying landscape. danger zone transition modes. agent mid-implementation “notices” something improved, refactored, redesigned — acts without stopping — ’s catastrophic failures originate. engineer picks architect’s pen without putting wrench.

title: Safeguards
text: Never switch modes without commit boundary. ’re Engineer Mode see something needs Architect Mode thinking: commit current work first, switch. ’re Architect Mode want start implementing: get explicit approval first, switch. catch thinking “’m …” “also noticed…” — ’s mode switch happening. Stop. Commit. Ask.

title: Safeguards
text: Run touching one file: uncommitted changes previous session, touch . Report user ask proceed.
code: 1. git status                    — What's the current state? 2. git diff                      — Any uncommitted changes? 3. Commit everything clean       — No exceptions. Commit BEFORE starting. 4. Verify the build passes       — Don't start from a broken state. 5. State your scope explicitly   — "I am changing X, Y, Z. Nothing else."

title: Safeguards

title: Session Notes
text: Deliverable: NEW-46 — make geneDrop() reject duplicate animal IDs clear stop(), strict TDD (one RED→GREEN→REFACTOR cycle). (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-06-01. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: fix: (guard + test + roxygen/man + NEWS/CHANGELOG/BACKLOG) + close-(docs:). bug (audit mechanism WRONG — NEW-48 pattern, Learning #8a): audit (geneDrop.R:82-104) called NEW-46 “parent lookup rowname; duplicate ids → wrong values.” Empirically hard CRASH R/geneDrop.R:97 rownames(ped) <- ids: R’s .rowNamesDF<- rejects duplicate row.names (precursor warning "non-unique value setting 'row.names'", hard error "duplicate 'row.names' allowed") — silently mangle. crash happens “parent lookup rowname” (L118-119) list-keying (L117) audit fingered, downstream mechanisms preempted/unreachable. Reproduced via /tmp/new46_probe*.R. METHOD TRAP recorded (→ Learning #14a): probe v1 used tryCatch(expr, warning=, error=) — warning= handler short-circuited precursor warning hid terminal error (read “warning”); probe v2 (suppress/let-pass warning) revealed hard error. Always probe PAST precursor warning. Reachability — DOUBLY-masked; direct-geneDrop()-call (grep + deterministic probe; workflow): - Canonical qcStudbook → reportGV → geneDrop path = MASKED TWICE. qcStudbook calls removeDuplicates (qcStudbook.R:277) unique()-collapses identical rows stop()s mismatched dup ids; kinship() — called reportGV.R:81, geneDrop call reportGV.R:92 — already guards stop("id values must unique"). non-qc’d dup-id ped passed reportGV crashes kinship clear message, never reaching geneDrop’s L97. - Scope NARROWED geneDrop-grep, widened (contrast NEW-53). rownames(x) <- … inventory found sibling site reportGV.R:122 rownames(ped) <- ped$id, kinship (L81) crashes first → ’s unreachable--dups → reportGV change needed. grep proved scope rather guessing (NEW-53’s grep widened scope 3 sites; narrowed 1). contract decision (USER, package author): reject clear stop() — silent-dedup “tolerate exact-dup, stop mismatched.” Rationale (Learning #8b): today’s behavior already crash, clear stop() contract-preserving (currently-succeeding call changes; cryptic message improves) — NEW-48/NEW-45-reject side. unique-id domain unambiguous (unlike NEW-45’s sweep) two sibling functions (kinship, removeDuplicates) already enforce ; kinship’s "id values must unique" supplied message precedent. fix (minimal, 4 lines): R/geneDrop.R:96-100, immediately NEW-45 period guard: dupIds <- duplicated(.character(ids)); ((dupIds)) stop("geneDrop(): animal IDs must unique; ", "duplicated id(s): ", toString(unique(.character(ids)[dupIds]))). Fires rownames(ped) <- ids, precursor warning either. Style mirrors adjacent period guard (toString/unique/.character). TDD trail: RED — one test_that("geneDrop rejects duplicate animal IDs (NEW-46)") tests/testthat/test_geneDrop.R (NEW-45 block): expect_error(…, "must unique") (1) mismatched dup id (2) exact-duplicate row (pinning reject--dups), + -false-positive guard (unique ids c("","B","C") still return data.frame ids). 2 dup assertions FAILED right reason — backtrace pinned geneDrop.R:97:3 rownames<-/.rowNamesDF<-, actual message "duplicate 'row.names' allowed" ≠ "must unique" — guard PASSED. GREEN — 4-line guard; 15 geneDrop tests pass, precursor warnings gone. REFACTOR (doc-) — roxygen unique-id paragraph + @param ids “unique”; regen man/geneDrop.Rd; NEWS.md/NEWS.Rmd/CHANGELOG.md bullets; removed NEW-46 BACKLOG.md. Verification: full suite via test_dir = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 1971 passed (+4 = exactly new expectations; S13 ended 1967); zero non-e2e offenders; 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings (stop() trips existing test — Learning #6 corollary clear). Lint net-zero: one object_usage_linter warning hasInvalidIdChar (now L92) pre-existing S13, HEAD-verified via scoped git stash (warning HEAD’s L86 — ’s stale installed-namespace yet @noRd validator; Learning #7). devtools::document() regenerated man/geneDrop.Rd, NAMESPACE change. geneDrop @example runs clean (14 rows ). Key files: R/geneDrop.R:96-100 (guard) + roxygen L34-39 (unique-id note) + L41 (@param ids). tests/testthat/test_geneDrop.R (NEW-46 block, ~L119-161). man/geneDrop.Rd (regen). NEWS.md / NEWS.Rmd / CHANGELOG.md (NEW-46 entry). BACKLOG.md (NEW-46 removed). Throwaway probes (/tmp, committed): new46_probe.R, new46_probe2.R, new46_probe3.R. GOTCHAS next session: 1. audit’s “duplicate/corruption” claim hard CRASH (3rd time NEW-48/NEW-25). R rejects duplicate data.frame row.names — rownames(x) <- <non-unique vector> idiom CRASHES, mangle. reproducing, probe PAST precursor warning (tryCatch warning= error= hides error warning). (→ Learning #14a.) 2. Reachability can DOUBLY-masked. geneDrop’s dup-id crash unreachable pipeline removeDuplicates (qcStudbook) kinship’s guard (reportGV.R:81, geneDrop) catch . Trace upstream guards declaring item reachable. (→ Learning #14b.) 3. fix-grep can NARROW scope proving sibling site unreachable (reportGV.R:122), widen (NEW-53). Run idiom inventory either way. (→ Learning #14b.) 4. geneDrop now TWO upfront input guards (period hasInvalidIdChar, duplicate-id). share toString(unique(.character(ids)[mask])) message idiom — keep new id-validation guards consistent. 5. Suggested next = NEW-20 (delete dead makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R + fully-commented test — @noRd/live fn) NEW-16 (summarizeKinshipValues.R:105-106 — secondQuartile mislabeled minimum, fivenum()[1] [2]; + rbind--loop O(n²)). Remaining audit items + traps ACTIVE TASK . Tracker reconciliation (audit items vs GitHub #1–#38) still open. 6. geneDrop.R carries 1 pre-existing lint (object_usage_linter @noRd hasInvalidIdChar, stale installed namespace) — resolves package reinstalled; don’t “fix” . Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end--end — declared every phase, gated three transitions via AskUserQuestion + separate pre-RED contract decision; 2 RED assertions failed RIGHT reason (backtrace-confirmed crash site) -false-positive guard stayed green. (+) Empirically OVERTURNED audit counts (crash corruption; site rownames<- lookup) — caught probe-v1 error (warning-handler masked terminal error) probing past warning v2; honest adversarial self-correction (highest-value move). (+) grep+probe NARROWED scope geneDrop-proving reportGV sibling unreachable (kinship guards first) — evidence guessing. (+) Right-sized orchestration: mechanism (deterministic base-R crash) + reachability (double-masked) + domain (two sibling guards) “already verified” → honest -workflow (5th consecutive; ultracode’s “unless already verified”). fan-re-run deterministic probe. (+) HEAD-verified lint pre-existing (Learning #7) rather assuming. (+) Contract-preserving stop() per Learning #8b, mirroring kinship’s message. (−) run full devtools::check() (e2e create_test_app baseline noise — S7/S9/S11/S13 precedent); mitigated test_dir 0/0 + net-zero lint + clean document() + @example run. (−) Phase 3E: input-validation guard compute function — app-startup/registration/dispatch/config behavior changed, live-browser launch apply; verified function/test level (RED proved crash, GREEN proved guard). FM #24 — stated, silently skipped. (−) 4 user interactions (1 contract decision + 3 phase gates) — mandated strict-TDD convention, recurring minor.

title: Session Notes
text: Deliverable: NEW-45 — enforce + document “IDs may contain period (.)” rule, strict TDD across two RED→GREEN→REFACTOR cycles. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-31. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 5e228bd9 (fix: enforcement + tests), 2527d6bc (docs: rationale), ceab030f (feat: error-renderer display) + close-(docs:). bug (audit mechanism CORRECT time): geneDrop rebuilds id/parent output columns strsplit(rownames(alleles), ".", fixed=TRUE) key[1]/key[2], rownames <id>.<sire|dam>. id containing . (e.g. ".1"→".1.sire") splits c("","1","sire") → id truncated "", sire/dam distinction lost. Empirically reproduced (/tmp/new45_probe.R). KEY decision — DOMAIN, mechanism (Learning #13): user GATED “fix--support periods” approach (A2) whether ID domain precludes .. read-multi-modal domain-sweep workflow (wf_21efc084-596; 4 searchers — validation-code ‖ docs ‖ separator/format ‖ example-data — + adversarial synthesis) returned verdict PRECLUDED, decisive evidence verified firsthand: inst/extdata/ui_guidance/input_format.html:86-97 documents id/sire/dam (L251 genotype-id) “Alphanumeric characters (symbols)”, live app via R/modInput.R:152-153, vignette manual_components/_input.Rmd:39-40 names tab authority. Reinforced deliberate authorial contrast (allele cols L141-150 explicitly allow “underscores, spaces dashes” — withheld ids). correctness-aligned fix ENFORCE documented--unenforced rule (reject), re-engineer parsing support .. Caveat synthesis flagged: rule documentation-(code enforced ), enforcing deliberate behavior change — author (user) signed . User decisions: (1) approach = B-broad (geneDrop guard + qcStudbook validation), (2) .-enforcement (full alphanumeric — lower risk; real data may legitimately use -/_), (3) document feature enforced data-input + auto-gen IDs (rationale: . breaks across software environments), (4) NEW-46 scope, (5) Phase-3E gap → extend now add error display. Cycle 1 — enforcement (5e228bd9 + docs 2527d6bc): - R/hasInvalidIdChar.R (NEW, @noRd): !.na(ids) & grepl(".", ids, fixed=TRUE) — single definition rule, reused everywhere (avoids guard-triplication audit dings elsewhere). - R/qcStudbook.R:180-189 (unknown2NA, addUIds): validates c(id,sire,dam); reportErrors=FALSE → stop(), reportErrors=TRUE → errorLst$invalidIdChars. (Restructured satisfy unnecessary_nesting_linter.) - R/geneDrop.R:76-80: stop() guard .character(ids) (defense--depth — GV Shiny module reaches geneDrop without passing qcStudbook). - R/getEmptyErrorLst.R + R/checkErrorLst.R: add & count new invalidIdChars field. - Generators (addUIds U####, obfuscateId) already period-free → locked characterization tests (pass & ). - Two pre-existing tests change disturbed (Learning #6 corollary, expected): test_getFocalAnimalPed.R:112 errorLst field-count 9→10; test_modGeneticValue.R special-char fixture C.003→C-003 (passing silently-corrupted geneDrop output — NEW-45 wild!) + new period-reject assertion. - Docs: roxygen qcStudbook/geneDrop/addUIds/obfuscateId (+ regen 4 .Rd), live input_format.html spec (cells + new <li> rationale note), NEWS.md/NEWS.Rmd, CHANGELOG.md. Cycle 2 — error display / Phase 3E (ceab030f): two QC error renderers enumerate fields hardcoded, new invalidIdChars wasn’t shown users (’d see error state without reason). Added invalidIdChars case R/processQcStudbookResult.R (Shiny error table; toString avoid adding 13 pre-existing lints — issue #30) R/summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R (addErrTxt summary line) + regen processQcStudbookResult.Rd. Verification: full suite via test_dir = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 1967 passed (+24 net across cycles); zero non-e2e offenders; 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings. Lint net-zero every edited file (processQcStudbookResult.R stays 13 pre-existing lints). devtools::document() regenerated 5 intended .Rd, NAMESPACE change (hasInvalidIdChar @noRd). Ran 4 changed exported functions’ examples clean (check()-equivalent slice). HEAD-baseline-verified regression offenders via git stash (passed HEAD → genuinely mine, expected). Key files: R/hasInvalidIdChar.R (validator), R/qcStudbook.R:180-189, R/geneDrop.R:76-80, R/getEmptyErrorLst.R (invalidIdChars field), R/checkErrorLst.R, R/processQcStudbookResult.R (period block), R/summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R (addErrTxt). Tests: test_hasInvalidIdChar.R (NEW), test_geneDrop.R, test_qcStudbook.R (pedPeriod fixture), test_addUIds.R/test_obfuscateId.R (guarantees), test_modGeneticValue.R (swap+reject), test_getFocalAnimalPed.R (9→10), test_modInput_qcStudbook.R + test_summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R (display). Workflow artifact: …/tasks/wixjhggi7.output (domain verdict). Probes (throwaway, /tmp, committed): new45_probe.R. GOTCHAS next session: 1. audit mechanism CORRECT, live decision often shifts DOMAIN-DEFINITION question — consult project’s documented spec (input_format.html, live via modInput) fix--support vs reject. read-multi-modal domain sweep right tool. (→ Learning #13.) 2. new errorLst field 4-touch change, 1: add getEmptyErrorLst + checkErrorLst count + hardcoded renderers (processQcStudbookResult.R, summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R) — Phase 3E catches renderer gap. trips structure-count tests (getFocalAnimalPed 9→10). 3. processQcStudbookResult.R 13 pre-existing lints (issue #30) — use toString() (paste(.., collapse=)) new line avoid adding debt. 4. Enforcement .-, full “symbols” — -/_/space// ids still pass (see test_modGeneticValue fixture). future session enforce full alphanumeric spec, ’s bigger behavior change (reject dash/underscore ids) — decision. 5. Suggested next = NEW-46 (geneDrop.R:82-104 duplicate-id rowname lookup — sibling NEW-45 consensus issue #7) NEW-20 (delete dead makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R). Tracker reconciliation (audit items vs GitHub #1–#38) still open. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end--end across two cycles — declared every phase, gated every transition via AskUserQuestion; RED tests failed right reason -false-positive guards passing; HEAD-baseline-verified two regression offenders rather assuming (Learning #3). (+) domain sweep highest-value move — user gated A2 , found decisive input_format.html spec, correctly pivoting fix support→reject; verified load-bearing evidence firsthand (Learning #20). (+) Right-sized orchestration: workflow genuine multi-location domain question; grep/probe (workflow) mechanism. (+) Surfaced counter-evidence (test_modGeneticValue passing corrupted output) Phase-3E renderer gap honestly rather silently shipping. (+) DRY — one validator, reused 3 enforcement points + 2 renderers. (+) Lint net-zero using codebase-idiomatic toString instead matching file’s dirty paste(collapse=) pattern. (−) run full devtools::check() (e2e create_test_app baseline noise makes clean signal impractical — S7/S9/S11 precedent); mitigated test_dir 0/0 + lint + clean document() + running 4 changed examples. FM #24: runtime verified testServer level (modGeneticValueServer rejects period-ids; two renderers display ), though launch live browser. title: Technical-Debt & Refactoring-Viability Audit — nprcgenekeepr
text: Across 11 functional areas audited, confirmed findings cluster around handful recurring themes: () Two coexisting, diverging Shiny applications — legacy monolith inst/application/ (server.R, ui.R) alongside newer modular Shiny modules R/. Logic maintained places, creating duplicate-maintenance tax standing risk two implementations drift apart. (b) Dead-code duplicate-variant accumulation — near-identical helper variants copy-pasted loop/aggregation bodies recur across kinship, genetic-value, group-formation cross-cutting clusters, inflating surface area review cost. (c) Hardcoded domain constants central schema / species profile — sex codes (M/F/U/H), minParentAge = 2, column-name lists embedded inline many call sites instead sourced one schema species-configuration object, blocks reuse species makes validation rules hard evolve. (d) Shiny concerns leaking core compute functions — UI / reactive / notification logic appears inside functions pure compute, coupling analytical core presentation layer hurting testability script-mode reuse. (e) Inconsistent error / return conventions — mix stop()/warning()/silent-NULL/return-value styles across QC pedigree code makes caller-side handling unpredictable. 60 confirmed findings across 11 functional areas (13 complexity, 19 duplication, 28 extensibility); 44 quick wins, 16 architectural overhauls.

Fold three column lists profile’s schema. Lowest-risk config items. Verifier: low-severity: passed without adversarial check

text: Findings generated parallel per-cluster auditors (11 clusters: QC, KIN, PED, GV, LOOP, GRP, GENO, APP, MISC, XDRY, XARCH), reading actual source functional area. Every medium/high finding underwent adversarial per-finding verification real code; verifier-CONFIRMED findings appear main sections, verifier-ADJUSTED severity category. title: Technical-Debt & Refactoring-Viability Audit — nprcgenekeepr
text: limitations audit run, conclusions code: PED (Pedigree Construction & Recoding) GV (Genetic Value Analysis & Reporting) produced ZERO findings auditor agents returned result — two functional areas effectively audited. Absence findings means “examined,” “clean.” re-run treating audit complete. Core files left unaudited include R/createPed*.R, R/recodePed*/addParents.R/addBackSecondParents.R (PED) R/reportGV.R, R/calcGU.R, R/rankSubjects.R, R/makeGeneticSummaryTable.R (GV) — though reportGV.R partially covered via XARCH cross-cutting findings. Appendix-B rejections caused transient tool/environment failures, verifier agent literally read file (“every tool call returned empty output”) therefore defaulted confirmed per fail-safe rule. substantive refutations — unverified, several likely real. Notably APP-11 (two parallel app implementations) rejected tooling failure independently CONFIRMED XARCH-1; GENO-2 (magic 10000L allele offset), APP-2/4/5 (monolith server complexity/magic strings), GRP-1/2/6 fall category worth re-verifying clean run. Net: treat 60 confirmed findings high-confidence floor, ceiling. targeted re-run PED, GV, tooling-failed verifications likely add findings.

Genome uniqueness values calculated using gene-drop simulation according MacCluer et al. (1986) Ballou & Lacy (1995), assigning unique alleles pedigree founders, simulating segregation throughout pedigree according Mendelian rules. Genome uniqueness measure probability animal possesses founder alleles present x animals (usually 0-3), thus rare risk lost population. range 2 100,000 simulations may selected. minimum 1,000 simulations recommended. genome uniqueness threshold value 0-3 also selected, desired. Select Begin Analysis button start gene dropping process, can monitor progress meter lower right corner display. aware systematic study pedigree structure algorithm performed extensive studies pedigrees various structures, 1000 iterations seemed provide reproducible results pedigrees. must emphasized pedigree structure expected affect precision iterations held constant.  soon calculations completed, table showing results analysis displayed 10 rows. calculations 1000 iterations gene dropping algorithm took 1 minute 38 seconds example pedigree 3,691 animals using MacBook Pro (Mid 2014), 2.8 GHz Intel Core i7 16 GB 1600 MHz DDR3 memory. can select many rows display changing values Show entries selection tool, may view results specific set animals entering IDs box . default display analysis list focal animals.  Searching table results Value Designation column can see starting row 268 values change High Value Low Value. Though shown , value Undetermined Value Designation column means animal parentage information. Infants young animals without assigned parents given “Undetermined” designation. Founders also parentage information high value definition.

text: pedigrees can imported using either Excel worksheets text files contain pedigree information using either Excel worksheets text files contain list focal animals remainder pedigree information pulled LabKey API. tutorial use pedigree file can created using makeExamplePedigreeFile function shown . function makeExamplePedigreeFile saves file returns full path name saved file, saving variable pedigreeFile. Note: user select store file. writes ExamplePedigree.csv place select within file system. use file name provided makeExamplePedigreeFile function tell read.table file read. Note number rows read. row represents individual within pedigree. next step put information read file pedigree object. done qcStudbook function, examines file contents tests common pedigree errors. can see errors can detected qcStudbook returning empty error list getEmptyErrorLst(). showing output function call now later tutorial explore errors depth. qcStudbook can take four arguments sb, minParentAge (years), reportChanges, reportErrors. However, sb default values sb argument required. prudent ensure parents least breeding age, species specific. used minParentAge 2 years.1 qcStudbook reports error, change call adding reportErrors argument set TRUE examine returned object. presented Pedigree Errors section.
code: library(nprcgenekeepr) pedigreeFile <- makeExamplePedigreeFile() breederPedCsv <- read.table(pedigreeFile,   sep = ",", header = TRUE,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  breederPedCsv$fromCenter <- "TRUE" breederPedCsv$fromCenter[   suppressWarnings(sample(     which(is.na(breederPedCsv$sire) &       is.na(breederPedCsv$dam)),     round(0.8 * length(which(       is.na(breederPedCsv$sire) &         is.na(breederPedCsv$dam)     )))   )) ] <- "FALSE" nrow(breederPedCsv) ## [1] 3694 breederPed <- qcStudbook(breederPedCsv, minParentAge = 2L)

code: focalAnimals <- breederPed$id[!(is.na(breederPed$sire) &   is.na(breederPed$dam)) &   is.na(breederPed$exit)] print(stri_c(   "There are ", length(focalAnimals),   " animals in the vector _focalAnimals_." )) breederPed[breederPed$id %in%   focalAnimals, c("id", "sire", "dam", "exit")][1L:10L, ] ##          id   sire    dam exit ## 1669 01QRQ4 VDBGDP TH7HTY <NA> ## 1743 CLSVU6 ULV9M7 SUFWJI <NA> ## 1887 1SPLS8 U9APLW 142GKP <NA> ## 1934 5IAFMK U4YSS5 WVE6Y4 <NA> ## 2072 HLQ9SY UI3RFL VEWC1E <NA> ## 2234 XFWVVX U3MFSZ L4LM1F <NA> ## 2337 6X6BG9 ENI6HX IUF0HC <NA> ## 2377 B228Q6 UEUIRJ CBSIAA <NA> ## 2378 B2CKHA ENI6HX WBFBR5 <NA> ## 2383 BCJJKN UA379T JPVAT3 <NA> breederPed <- setPopulation(ped = breederPed, ids = focalAnimals) nrow(breederPed[breederPed$population, ]) ## [1] 327 trimmedPed <- trimPedigree(focalAnimals, breederPed) nrow(breederPed) ## [1] 3694 nrow(trimmedPed) ## [1] 704 trimmedPedInformative <- trimPedigree(focalAnimals, breederPed,   removeUninformative = TRUE ) nrow(trimmedPedInformative) ## [1] 509 nonfocalInTrimmedPed <- trimmedPed$id[!trimmedPed$id %in% focalAnimals] length(nonfocalInTrimmedPed) ## [1] 377 allFocalParents <- c(   breederPed$sire[breederPed$id %in% focalAnimals],   breederPed$dam[breederPed$id %in% focalAnimals] ) trimmedFocalParents <- c(   trimmedPed$sire[trimmedPed$id %in% focalAnimals],   trimmedPed$dam[trimmedPed$id %in% focalAnimals] ) all.equal(allFocalParents, trimmedFocalParents) # Are the IDs the same? ## [1] TRUE notFocalNotParent <-   trimmedPed$id[!trimmedPed$id %in% c(focalAnimals, allFocalParents)] length(notFocalNotParent) ## [1] 187 unknownBirth <- breederPed$id[is.na(breederPed$birth)] knownExit <- breederPed$id[!is.na(breederPed$exit)] unknownBirthKnownExit <-   breederPed$id[is.na(breederPed$birth) | !is.na(breederPed$exit)] knownPed <- breederPed[!breederPed$id %in% unknownBirthKnownExit, ] otherIds <- knownPed$id[!knownPed$id %in% trimmedPed$id[is.na(trimmedPed$exit)]] print(stri_c(   "The living animals in the pedigree that are not in the trimmed ",   "pedigree are ", get_and_or_list(otherIds), "." ))

title: Interactive Use of nprcgenekeepr
text: genetic value analysis must carefully performed. next three commands set entire pedigree analysis. first three commands set pedigree members part population interest setting population column TRUE individuals. Note new pedigree object (ped) created. arguments reportGV optional except ped, may often want non-default values. ped Pedigree information data.frame format guIter Integer indicating number iterations gene-drop analysis. Default 5000 iterations guThresh Integer indicating threshold number animals defining unique allele. Default considers allele “unique” found 1 animal. pop Character vector animal IDs consider population interest. default NULL. byID Logical variable length 1 passed eventually used alleleFreq(), calculates count allele provided vector. byID TRUE ids provided, function count unique alleles individual (homozygous alleles counted 1). happens limit analysis trimmed pedigree? Remember trimmed pedigree still contains genetic information full pedigree focal animals. clear limiting analysis animals interest reduces effort required examine animals interest.
code: ped <- setPopulation(breederPed, NULL) probands <- ped$id[ped$population] ped <- trimPedigree(probands, ped,   removeUninformative = FALSE,   addBackParents = FALSE ) geneticValue <- reportGV(ped,   guIter = 50L,   guThresh = 3L,   byID = TRUE,   updateProgress = NULL ) summary(geneticValue) ## The genetic value report  ## Individuals in Pedigree: 3694  ## Male Founders: 141 ## Female Founders: 122 ## Total Founders: 263  ## Founder Equivalents: 241.84  ## Founder Genome Equivalents: 164.01  ## Live Offspring: 4052  ## High Value Individuals: 2947  ## Low Value Individuals: 747 trimmedGeneticValue <- reportGV(trimmedPed,   guIter = 50L,   guThresh = 3L,   byID = TRUE,   updateProgress = NULL ) summary(trimmedGeneticValue) ## The genetic value report  ## Individuals in Pedigree: 327  ## Male Founders: 3 ## Female Founders: 17 ## Total Founders: 20  ## Founder Equivalents: 109.67  ## Founder Genome Equivalents: 47.49  ## Live Offspring: 321  ## High Value Individuals: 226  ## Low Value Individuals: 101

title: Interactive Use of nprcgenekeepr
text: names object within genetic value report object (trimmedGeneticValue) can listed shown next line code. report object (R dataframe) can -turn examined. report conveniently used separate object. next section code rounds numerical values converts columns characters display table first 10 lines included. start next lines code getting fresh copy genetic value report since changed numeric values characters last section print table. lines demonstrate one way extracting component objects genetic value object. informative examine distribution genetic uniqueness, mean kinship, z-scores (normalized mean kinship values). Creation boxplot genetic uniqueness values shown .  Extraction individual mean kinship values corresponding z-scores shown next code chunk. Creation boxplots mean kinship z-scores left exercise.
code: names(trimmedGeneticValue) ##  [1] "report"          "kinship"         "gu"              "fe"              ##  [5] "fg"              "maleFounders"    "femaleFounders"  "nMaleFounders"   ##  [9] "nFemaleFounders" "total" names(trimmedGeneticValue$report) ## column names ##  [1] "id"              "sex"             "age"             "birth"           ##  [5] "exit"            "population"      "origin"          "indivMeanKin"    ##  [9] "zScores"         "gu"              "totalOffspring"  "livingOffspring" ## [13] "value"           "rank" nrow(trimmedGeneticValue$report) ## Number of rows ## [1] 327 rpt <- trimmedGeneticValue[["report"]] rpt$indivMeanKin <- round(rpt$indivMeanKin, 5L) rpt$zScores <- round(rpt$zScores, 2L) rpt$gu <- round(rpt$gu, 5L) rpt <- toCharacter(rpt) names(rpt) <- headerDisplayNames(names(rpt)) knitr::kable(rpt[1L:10L, ]) # needs more work for display purposes. rpt <- trimmedGeneticValue[["report"]] kmat <- trimmedGeneticValue[["kinship"]] f <- trimmedGeneticValue[["total"]] mf <- trimmedGeneticValue[["maleFounders"]] ff <- trimmedGeneticValue[["femaleFounders"]] nmf <- trimmedGeneticValue[["nMaleFounders"]] nff <- trimmedGeneticValue[["nFemaleFounders"]] fe <- trimmedGeneticValue[["fe"]] fg <- trimmedGeneticValue[["fg"]] gu <- rpt[, "gu"] guBox <- ggplot(data.frame(gu = gu), aes(x = "", y = gu)) +   geom_boxplot(     color = "darkblue",     fill = "lightblue",     notch = TRUE, #| fig.alt: >     #|   Histogram of time between eruptions for Old Faithful.     #|   It is a bimodal distribution with peaks at 50-55 and     #|   80-90 minutes.      outlier.color = "red",     outlier.shape = 1L   ) +   theme_classic() +   geom_jitter(width = 0.2) +   coord_flip() +   ylab("Score") +   ggtitle("Genetic Uniqueness") print(guBox) mk <- rpt[, "indivMeanKin"] zs <- rpt[, "zScores"]

title: Interactive Use of nprcgenekeepr
text: primary purpose nprcgenekeepr form breeding groups according best information regarding maintaining genetic characteristics desire realities associated animal husbandry needs. several options must consider forming groups using nprcgenekeepr, examine using code . high-value: Randomly select high-value animals genetic value analysis : Randomly select animals genetic value analysis candidates: Use candidate animals entered form groups Randomly assign animals without regard sex Use harem structure one breeding male per group Specify sex ratio 0.5 10 (F/M) Whether pre-populate (seed) groups animals choice Number groups formed Whether ignore females minimum parent age Number simulations used search optimal group makeup Whether kinship coefficients included results decisions regarding options expressed call function groupAddAssign. complete description function arguments available using code shown . descriptions function parameters extracted documentation near time tutorial prepared. candidates Character vector IDs animals available use forming groups. animals may present currentGroups included within candidates. currentGroups List character vectors IDs animals currently assigned groups. Defaults list character(0) sub-list element (one group formed) assuming groups pre-populated. kmat Numeric matrix pairwise kinship values. Rows columns named animal IDs. ped Dataframe ‘Pedigree’. contains pedigree information including IDs listed candidates. threshold Numeric value indicating minimum kinship level considered group formation. Pairwise kinship level ignored. default values 0.015625. ignore List character vectors representing sex combinations ignored. provided, vectors list specify pairwise kinship ignored certain sexes. Default ignore pairwise kinship females. minAge Integer value indicating minimum age consider group formation. Pairwise kinships involving animal age younger ignored. Default 1 year. iter Integer indicating number times perform random group formation process. Default value 1000 iterations. numGp Integer value indicating number groups formed list IDs. Default 1. updateProgress Function NULL. function defined, called iteration update shiny::Progress object. harem Logical variable set TRUE, formed groups single male least minAge old. sexRatio Numeric value indicating ratio females males x 0.5 20 increments 0.5. withKin Logical variable set TRUE, kinship matrix group returned along group score. Defaults return kinship matrix. maintains compatibility earlier versions. use trimmedPed pedigree code. illustration purposes going keep numbers candidates, groups, iterations fairly small. get first animal IDs use candidates selecting animals least 2 years old time pedigree sampled (01-01-2015). candidates made 184 females 96 males. parameters currentGroups, threshold, ignore, minAge, sexRatio, withKin, updateProgress allowed take default values. setting sexRatio parameter 0 ignored following call groupAddAssign function. consistent value 0 making little since breeding colony. empty seventh group bottom evidence candidate animals placed group without exceeding default value 0.015625.
code: ?groupAddAssign candidates <- trimmedPed$id[trimmedPed$birth < as.Date("2013-01-01") &   !is.na(trimmedPed$birth) &   is.na(trimmedPed$exit)] table(trimmedPed$sex[trimmedPed$id %in% candidates]) ##  ##   F   M   H   U  ## 184  96   0   0

code: names(getEmptyErrorLst()) ##  [1] "failedDatabaseConnection" "missingColumns"           ##  [3] "invalidDateRows"          "suspiciousParents"        ##  [5] "femaleSires"              "maleDams"                 ##  [7] "sireAndDam"               "duplicateIds"             ##  [9] "invalidIdChars"           "changedCols" knitr::kable(nprcgenekeepr::pedOne) pedOne <- nprcgenekeepr::pedOne # put it in the local environment ped <- qcStudbook(pedOne, minParentAge = 0.0) ## Error in `qcStudbook()`: ## ! Parents with low age at birth of offspring are listed in /tmp/RtmpgU5bGm/lowParentAge.csv. pedOne$birth_date[pedOne$ego_id == "o4"] <- as.Date("2015-09-16") pedOne$birth_date[pedOne$ego_id == "d2"] <- as.Date("2006-04-13") ped <- qcStudbook(pedOne, minParentAge = 0.0) ped[ped$id %in% c("s2", "d2", "o3", "o4"), ] ##   id sire  dam sex gen      birth exit  age recordStatus ## 2 d2 <NA> <NA>   F   0 2006-04-13 <NA> 20.2     original ## 4 s2 <NA> <NA>   M   0 2006-06-19 <NA> 20.0     original ## 7 o3   s2   d2   F   1 2012-04-11 <NA> 14.2     original ## 8 o4   s2   d2   M   1 2015-09-16 <NA> 10.7     original errorList <- qcStudbook(pedOne,   minParentAge = 0.0, reportChanges = TRUE,   reportErrors = TRUE ) summary(errorList) ## Error: The animal listed as a sire and also listed as a female is: s1. ## Error: The animal listed as a dam and also listed as a male is: d1. ## Change: The column where space was removed is: si re to sire. ## Change: The columns where underscore was removed are: ego_id, dam_id, and birth_date to egoid, damid, and birthdate. ## Change: The column changed from: egoid to id. ## Change: The column changed from: damid to dam. ## Change: The column changed from: birthdate to birth. ##  ## Please check and correct the pedigree file. ## pedOne <- nprcgenekeepr::pedOne errorList <- qcStudbook(pedOne,   minParentAge = 0L, reportChanges = FALSE,   reportErrors = TRUE ) options(width = 90L) summary(errorList) ## Error: The animal listed as a sire and also listed as a female is: s1. ## Error: The animal listed as a dam and also listed as a male is: d1. ##  ## Please check and correct the pedigree file. ##   ## Animal records where parent records are suspicous because of dates. ## One or more parents appear too young at time of birth. ##   dam sire id sex      birth recordStatus exit  sireBirth   damBirth sireAge damAge ## 2  d2   s1 o2   F 2009-03-17     original <NA> 2000-07-18 2015-09-16    8.66   -6.5 ## 3  d2   s2 o3   F 2012-04-11     original <NA> 2006-06-19 2015-09-16    5.81   -3.4 ## 4  d2   s2 o4   M 2006-04-13     original <NA> 2006-06-19 2015-09-16   -0.18   -9.4
